exercises.title: The paper as a structured section graph
exercises.prompt: Treat a research paper as a structured artifact with a known section graph, not freeform prose. Build the section graph (abstract, intro, method, results, related-work, conclusion) with dependencies (results needs method), and show validating that a draft has all required sections in valid order. Discuss why structure-first beats prose-first.
exercises.hint: Section graph: nodes with dependencies (e.g. results depends on method, conclusion on results). Validate a draft has all sections + dependencies satisfied. Show a complete graph valid, a missing-method draft invalid. Structure-first: catch missing/misordered sections before writing prose.

exercises.title: Generate a LaTeX skeleton with declared slots
exercises.prompt: Generate a LaTeX skeleton that declares its abstract, sections, figure slots, and bibliography keys before any prose. Build a skeleton generator producing valid LaTeX structure with placeholder slots, and show the skeleton declaring figure slots and bib keys. Discuss why declaring slots upfront is a contract.
exercises.hint: Generate LaTeX: \begin{abstract}...\end{abstract}, \section{} per section, \includegraphics slots, \cite{} keys declared. Placeholders to fill later. Show the skeleton with figure slots + bib keys. Declared slots = a contract: the prose-filler must satisfy every declared slot.

exercises.title: Validate compilation — a broken contract fails loud
exercises.prompt: If the contract is broken the document does not compile, and the failure is loud. Build a pre-compile validator that checks the filled paper against the skeleton contract — every slot filled, every cited key defined in the bibliography, every figure slot has a figure — and flags violations BEFORE running LaTeX. Show a valid paper passing and a broken one (undefined citation) failing loud. Discuss why loud failure beats silent corruption.
exercises.hint: Validate: all SLOT placeholders filled, all \cite keys present in the .bib, all figure slots have files. Flag any violation. Show valid passing, an undefined-citation paper failing with the specific violation. Loud failure: a broken contract is caught explicitly, not silently mis-rendered.
